content.problemStatement:
The assignment operator (`=`) in Java is used to assign a value to a variable. \
It stores the right-hand side (RHS) value into the left-hand side (LHS) variable. \
The data type of both sides must be compatible, or **type conversion** must be applied.

**Syntax:**
```java
variableName = value;
```
- The **variable** on the left-hand side (`variableName`) stores the assigned value.
- The **value** on the right-hand side (`value`) is assigned to the variable.

---

**Example:**
```java
class Seedit {
    public static void main(String[] args) {
        int number; // Declaration
        number = 10; // Assignment

        System.out.println("Assigned Value: " + number);
    }
}
```

**Output:**
```default
Assigned Value: 10
```
